seo.title: RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization in Agno Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix rag retrieval quality optimization in Agno agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Agno agents, RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization issues manifest as poor chunking strategies, irrelevant retrieved context, and embedding mismatches that degrade response accuracy in multi-modal and agentic workflows. These problems are amplified by Agno's lightweight, model-agnostic design where default vector stores and retrievers often fail to handle complex knowledge bases effectively. Symptoms appear as hallucinated responses, missed key information, and inconsistent retrieval across agent sessions.

howItManifests.scenario: Default chunking strategy on technical docs
howItManifests.symptom: Agents retrieve incomplete code snippets or split function definitions
howItManifests.impact: Generated solutions contain syntax errors and fail execution

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-modal RAG with mixed text/image knowledge base
howItManifests.symptom: Text queries retrieve irrelevant images or vice versa
howItManifests.impact: Agent responses ignore visual context entirely

howItManifests.scenario: Agent session with evolving knowledge base
howItManifests.symptom: Cached embeddings return outdated document chunks
howItManifests.impact: Agent provides contradictory information across conversations

howItManifests.scenario: Team agents sharing single vector store
howItManifests.symptom: Specialized sub-agents retrieve generic irrelevant context

detection.name: Retrieval Relevance Score Monitoring
detection.description: Logs cosine similarity scores for top-k retrieved chunks to detect low-quality matches
detection.code:
from agno import Agent
from agno.retrievers import Vector
from vex import verify

@verify()
def rag_agent(query: str):
    agent = Agent(
        retriever=Vector(collection_name='docs'),
        model='gpt-4o-mini'
    )
    response = agent.run(query)
    
    # Extract retrieval scores from agent context
    retrieval_scores = [ctx.metadata.get('similarity_score', 0) 
                       for ctx in agent.context.retrieved_docs]
    avg_relevance = sum(retrieval_scores) / len(retrieval_scores)
    
    assert avg_relevance > 0.75, f"Low retrieval relevance: {avg_relevance:.3f}"
    return response

rag_agent('How to implement semantic search?')

detection.name: Context Coverage Analysis
detection.description: Verifies retrieved chunks contain all query keywords to detect incomplete retrieval
detection.code:
def detect_context_coverage(query: str, retrieved_docs: list) -> bool:
    query_words = set(query.lower().split())
    doc_content = ' '.join([doc.page_content.lower() for doc in retrieved_docs])
    covered_words = query_words.intersection(doc_content.split())
    coverage_ratio = len(covered_words) / len(query_words)
    return coverage_ratio > 0.8

@verify()
def coverage_agent(query: str):
    from agno import Agent
    agent = Agent(retriever=Vector())
    response = agent.run(query)
    
    coverage = detect_context_coverage(query, agent.context.retrieved_docs)
    assert coverage, "Retrieved context misses key query terms"
    return response

detection.name: Hallucination Detection via Ground Truth Check
detection.description: Compares agent response against retrieved context to detect fabrications
detection.code:
from difflib import SequenceMatcher

def hallucination_score(response: str, context_docs: list) -> float:
    context_text = ' '.join([doc.page_content for doc in context_docs])
    similarity = SequenceMatcher(None, response.lower(), context_text.lower()).ratio()
    return similarity

@verify()
def anti_hallucination_agent(query: str):
    from agno import Agent
    agent = Agent(retriever=Vector())
    response = agent.run(query)
    
    hallucination_ratio = hallucination_score(response, agent.context.retrieved_docs)
    assert hallucination_ratio > 0.6, f"High hallucination detected: {hallucination_ratio:.3f}"
    return response

fixes.name: Semantic Chunking with Code-Aware Splitting
fixes.description: Implements custom chunker that preserves code blocks and function boundaries
fixes.code:
from agno.retrievers import Vector
from langchain.text_splitter import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter

code_aware_splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter(
    chunk_size=1000,
    chunk_overlap=200,
    separators=['\n\n', '\n## ', '\ndef ', '\nclass ', '\nimport ', '\n', ' '],
    keep_separator=True
)

rag_agent = Agent(
    retriever=Vector(
        collection_name='code_docs',
        text_splitter=code_aware_splitter
    ),
    model='gpt-4o-mini',
    add_history_to_messages=True
)

# Usage
result = rag_agent.run('Show me the FastAPI dependency injection pattern')

fixes.name: Hybrid Dense + Sparse Retrieval
fixes.description: Combines semantic search with keyword matching for better recall
fixes.code:
from agno.retrievers import Vector
from langchain.retrievers import EnsembleRetriever
from langchain.retrievers import BM25Retriever

# Dense retriever (semantic)
dense_retriever = Vector(collection_name='docs')

# Sparse retriever (keyword)
sparse_retriever = BM25Retriever.from_documents(docs)

# Hybrid ensemble
hybrid_retriever = EnsembleRetriever(
    retrievers=[dense_retriever, sparse_retriever],
    weights=[0.7, 0.3]
)

agent = Agent(
    retriever=hybrid_retriever,
    model='gpt-4o-mini'
)

fixes.name: Multi-Query RAG with Hypothetical Questions
fixes.description: Generates multiple query variants to improve retrieval coverage
fixes.code:
from agno import Agent
from langchain.retrievers.multi_query import MultiQueryRetriever

multi_query_agent = Agent(
    retriever=MultiQueryRetriever.from_llm(
        retriever=Vector(collection_name='docs'),
        llm='gpt-4o-mini'
    ),
    model='gpt-4o-mini'
)

# Automatically generates 3 query variants:
# 1. Original: "How does JWT authentication work?"
# 2. Generated: "Explain JSON Web Token auth mechanism"
# 3. Generated: "JWT token validation process"

result = multi_query_agent.run('How does JWT authentication work?')

vexAutomation.heading: Automating RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automates RAG quality monitoring by wrapping Agno's agent.run() with the @vex.verify() decorator, continuously evaluating retrieval relevance, context coverage, and hallucination rates across all agent interactions. It establishes adaptive baselines from successful runs and flags regressions when retrieval quality drops below thresholds, providing detailed diagnostics including similarity scores and missing query terms. For remediation, Vex suggests specific fixes like hybrid retrieval or semantic chunking based on failure patterns observed in production traffic.

The integration requires only pip install agno vex-sdk and wrapping existing agent calls—no changes to core logic needed. Vex captures full context including retrieved documents, embeddings, and response artifacts for root-cause analysis, enabling teams to maintain RAG quality as knowledge bases evolve and agent workloads scale.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex import verify
from agno import Agent

@verify(
    checks=[
        'retrieval_relevance > 0.75',
        'context_coverage > 0.8',
        'hallucination_score < 0.4'
    ]
)
def production_rag_agent(query: str):
    """Production RAG agent with automated quality monitoring"""
    agent = Agent(
        retriever=Vector(collection_name='production_docs'),
        model='gpt-4o-mini',
        add_history_to_messages=True
    )
    return agent.run(query)

# Vex automatically:
# ✅ Monitors all metrics in real-time
# ✅ Alerts on quality degradation
# ✅ Suggests optimal retriever configs
# ✅ Tracks improvements over time

result = production_rag_agent('Explain Agno agent orchestration patterns')

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex today with pip install vex-sdk and wrap your Agno agents to eliminate RAG quality issues automatically.
